profile.name: Lt. Col. Charles Halt
roles: Senior military witness, Rendlesham Forest incident
profile.summary: Retired U.S. Air Force Lieutenant Colonel and Deputy Base Commander at RAF Bentwaters/Woodbridge who was the senior military officer witness to the Rendlesham Forest incident in December 1980 - widely regarded as the most well-documented military UAP encounter in history. He authored the official 'Halt Memo' to the UK Ministry of Defence and made a real-time audio recording of his unit's live encounter with anomalous lights on December 28, 1980. Halt has since stated publicly and under sworn affidavit that what he witnessed was non-human technology and that both the U.S. and UK governments covered up the incident.
early_career: Commissioned as U.S. Air Force officer in 1966
Served in multiple USAF assignments over a 28-year career
Rose to the rank of Lieutenant Colonel; assigned as Deputy Base Commander at the twin-base complex of RAF Bentwaters/Woodbridge in Suffolk, England

key_events.event: Commissioned as a U.S. Air Force officer.
Assigned as Deputy Base Commander at RAF Bentwaters/Woodbridge, Suffolk, England - a major USAF installation in the UK during the Cold War.

The Rendlesham Forest incident: on the night of December 27-28, Halt led a patrol into Rendlesham Forest to investigate anomalous lights reported by security personnel. His team observed unexplained aerial craft at close range. He recorded a real-time audio log documenting his unit's live observations throughout the encounter, capturing radiation meter readings, visual descriptions, and witness reactions as events unfolded.

Submitted the 'Halt Memo' - a formal memorandum to the UK Ministry of Defence documenting the Rendlesham Forest incident. The memo described anomalous lights, ground traces, radiation readings above background levels, and observations of structured craft. It became one of the most cited official UAP documents in history.

The Halt Memo was declassified and publicly released following a UK Freedom of Information Act request by researcher Dot Street and others. Its release confirmed that a senior U.S. military officer had officially reported a UAP encounter to the British government.
Retired from the U.S. Air Force after 28 years of service at the rank of Lieutenant Colonel.

Submitted a signed affidavit attesting to the reality of the Rendlesham Forest encounter and explicitly stating that the official explanations offered - including the Orfordness lighthouse and a passing meteor shower - were physically inconsistent with what he and his team observed.
Signed the Coalition for Freedom of Information affidavit alongside other senior military witnesses, formally placing his sworn testimony in the public record.

Testified at the Citizen Hearing on Disclosure at the National Press Club in Washington D.C., presenting his account before former members of the U.S. Congress and calling for full government transparency on the Rendlesham case.
Co-authored 'The Halt Perspective' with researcher John Hanson, providing the most comprehensive firsthand account of the Rendlesham Forest incident from Halt's perspective.

Gave extended interviews for the documentary 'The Rendlesham UFO Incident,' stating that classified aspects of the case have never been made public and that the full scope of what occurred over those nights remains officially suppressed.

claims.claim: The object observed during the Rendlesham Forest incident was non-human technology.
claims.notes: Halt personally witnessed and documented the encounter as the senior officer present. The Halt Memo is an authenticated government document. Radiation readings above background levels were recorded in real time and documented in the memo. Ground traces were found and measured. The audio recording captures Halt's unit observing objects that respond to their presence. However, no physical craft was officially retrieved or confirmed, and the British government concluded no security threat was identified.

claims.claim: Both the U.S. and UK governments covered up the true nature of the Rendlesham Forest incident.
claims.notes: Halt has stated this under oath in multiple affidavits and interviews. The original classification of the Halt Memo and the two-year delay before its public release under FOIA support some degree of official information control. However, no document explicitly confirming deliberate high-level suppression has been declassified. The UK government's position has been that no defense significance was identified.

claims.claim: The Halt audio recording is an authentic real-time documentation of a UAP observation.
claims.notes: The audio recording exists, is universally accepted as authentic, and has never been disputed as fabricated by any credible source. It documents Halt and his team tracking unexplained lights and taking radiation meter readings in real time. The recording is internally consistent with the Halt Memo and with independent witness accounts.

claims.claim: The official explanations for the Rendlesham incident - the Orfordness lighthouse and a passing meteor shower - are physically inconsistent with the evidence.
claims.notes: Halt and multiple independent researchers have documented specific reasons why these explanations fail: the lighthouse rotates on a known schedule inconsistent with the observed movement of the lights; the meteor explanation does not account for the multinight duration of the events or the close-range ground traces. The lighthouse explanation in particular has been publicly criticized by astronomers and radar analysts. The official explanations have not been formally withdrawn but are widely considered inadequate by researchers who have reviewed the evidence record.

supporting: Senior commissioned military officer with over 28 years of service; no identified personal motive for fabrication
Produced contemporaneous official documentation - the Halt Memo - in his capacity as Deputy Base Commander
Real-time audio recording of the encounter exists, is authenticated, and has never been disputed as fabricated
Ground traces and radiation readings above background levels were documented at the time by military personnel with instruments
Multiple independent witnesses among base personnel corroborate the broad facts of the incident
Submitted sworn affidavits placing his testimony in the legal record
The UK government's initial handling and classification of the Halt Memo suggests the incident was taken seriously at the time
Halt's military career was not derailed by his reporting, suggesting he was regarded as credible within his chain of command
contradicting: The Halt Memo was written approximately two weeks after the incident, not contemporaneously with events
Official UK investigations concluded no defense threat was identified and declined to pursue further inquiry
The Orfordness lighthouse is genuinely visible from Rendlesham Forest at night and could account for some observed light phenomena
Halt's public statements have grown more expansive and definitive over the decades since the 1981 memo
Witness accounts among base personnel contain internal inconsistencies and some details conflict between individuals
No physical evidence of the craft itself has been retrieved or independently confirmed

associated_people.name: John Burroughs
associated_people.role: U.S. Air Force Staff Sergeant; primary Rendlesham witness
associated_people.relationship: Junior airman under Halt's command who was the primary ground witness on the first night of the Rendlesham Forest incident; accompanied Halt during the December 28 patrol. Halt later advocated publicly for Burroughs' VA medical benefits case.

associated_people.name: Nick Pope
associated_people.role: Former UK MOD UFO desk officer; author
associated_people.relationship: Investigated the Rendlesham Forest case during his tenure at the UK Ministry of Defence UFO desk (1991-1994) and has publicly corroborated the credibility of Halt's account and the significance of the Halt Memo as an official government document.

associated_people.name: Larry Warren
associated_people.role: U.S. Air Force airman; Rendlesham claimant
associated_people.relationship: U.S. airman who claimed to be a witness to the second night of the Rendlesham encounter. A controversial figure whose account sometimes conflicts with Halt's on specific details, though both describe extraordinary craft. Halt has distanced himself from Warren's more embellished claims.

associated_people.name: Jim Penniston
associated_people.role: U.S. Air Force Staff Sergeant; first-night Rendlesham witness
associated_people.relationship: Staff Sergeant who was the primary witness on the first night of the Rendlesham incident and claims to have physically touched the landed craft. Collaborated with John Burroughs on public accounts. His narrative and Halt's account are largely complementary, covering different nights of the same event.

disclosures.title: The Halt Memo
disclosures.notes: Official memorandum submitted by Halt to the UK MOD describing the Rendlesham Forest incident in formal military terms. Covers ground traces, radiation readings above background levels, and structured craft observations. One of the most cited official UAP documents in history; declassified and publicly released in 1983 under a UK FOIA request.

disclosures.title: Coalition for Freedom of Information Affidavit
disclosures.notes: Signed sworn affidavit in which Halt attests to the reality of the Rendlesham Forest encounter and explicitly states that official explanations - including the lighthouse and meteor shower theories - are physically inconsistent with what he and his team directly observed. Signed alongside other senior military witnesses.

disclosures.title: Citizen Hearing on Disclosure Testimony
disclosures.notes: Testified before former members of the U.S. Congress at the Citizen Hearing on Disclosure, presenting his firsthand account of the Rendlesham Forest incident and calling for full government transparency. One of his most high-profile public appearances on the subject.

disclosures.title: The Halt Perspective
disclosures.notes: Co-authored with researcher John Hanson. The most comprehensive firsthand account of the Rendlesham Forest incident from Halt's perspective, covering events across all nights of the encounter and providing Halt's assessment of the official response and subsequent suppression.

career_connections.relationship: Senior investigating officer and primary military witness to the December 1980 encounter; commanded the patrol that observed anomalous craft on December 27-28 and authored the official government documentation of the events.
Burroughs was a junior airman under Halt's command and a key witness during the Rendlesham incident; Halt later advocated for Burroughs' VA medical benefits case related to radiation exposure.
Pope investigated the Rendlesham case for the UK Ministry of Defence UFO desk and has publicly corroborated the significance and authenticity of Halt's account and the Halt Memo.
Career military officer; served 28 years and retired as Lieutenant Colonel after a full career including command-level assignment at RAF Bentwaters/Woodbridge.

sources.title: The Halt Memo (January 1981, declassified 1983) - UK National Archives
sources.notes: Primary source - the authenticated official memorandum from Halt to the UK MOD. Available via the UK National Archives UFO file releases.

sources.title: Halt Audio Recording (December 28, 1980)
sources.notes: Contemporaneous real-time audio log recorded by Halt during the December 28 patrol. Widely available publicly and universally accepted as authentic.

sources.title: The Halt Perspective - Charles Halt and John Hanson (2016)
sources.notes: Co-authored firsthand account of the Rendlesham Forest incident from Halt's perspective (Haunted Skies Publishing, 2016).

sources.title: Citizen Hearing on Disclosure - Halt Testimony (2013)
sources.notes: Halt's formal testimony before former members of Congress at the National Press Club, April-May 2013.

sources.title: Coalition for Freedom of Information Affidavit (2010)
sources.notes: Halt's signed sworn affidavit attesting to the reality of the Rendlesham encounter and the falseness of official explanations.
